name: Sportradar API
what_it_does: Premium sports data platform providing live scores, statistics, odds, and push streaming for 50+ sports used by major sportsbooks and media companies.
use_cases: Building real-time sports scoreboards and live game trackers
Powering sportsbook odds feeds and betting platforms
Generating sports analytics dashboards and historical stats queries
Automating sports content creation with structured game data
Monitoring player and team performance metrics across seasons
not_for: Hobbyist or free-tier use — requires commercial contract and approval
Projects needing instant self-service API access without sales approval
best_when: Building production-grade sports applications that require reliable, real-time data with SLA guarantees and breadth across many sports and markets.
avoid_when: You need free or low-cost access, quick prototyping without approval, or only need data for a single minor sport.

auth.notes: API key passed as query parameter or header. Keys are scoped to specific feeds/sports packages purchased. Requires account approval before key issuance.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Trial access available for evaluation; limited endpoints and request volume
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise contracts priced by sport, feed type, and data volume; pricing not publicly listed — requires sales engagement
pricing.notes: Commercial contracts required for production use. Trial keys available for developers evaluating the platform. Major packages include NFL, NBA, MLB, NHL, soccer, and more.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by contract tier; push streaming via WebSocket reduces polling need

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST errors return structured JSON with codes; push stream error events are documented but reconnection logic is left to the client
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All endpoints are read-only data feeds; no mutation operations
known_agent_gotchas: API keys are scoped per sport/feed — agents must use the correct key for each endpoint
Push streaming requires WebSocket management; agents need reconnect logic
Trial keys expire and have endpoint restrictions not clearly flagged in responses
Rate limit headers not always consistently returned across all feed types

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced on all endpoints. API keys scoped to purchased packages, providing some granularity. No OAuth or JWT; key rotation process requires contacting support.
